challenge: Seasonal revenue swings — packed April through October, dead November through March
budget_range: I don't know, whatever makes sense
team_size: not sure how to answer that — it depends on the season
backstory: Keith Morlock runs Morlock Landscape and Design, a family-owned business in Naperville with over 35 years of experience. They do everything from mowing and maintenance to full design-build projects — outdoor kitchens, hardscaping, patios, fire pits. He's good at the work but has never thought strategically about marketing. He gives genuinely vague answers to budget and team questions — not articulate uncertainty, but actual 'I don't know' with no elaboration. He hasn't quantified these things and doesn't try to.
conversational_behaviors: Answers budget/team questions with genuine shrugs — 'I don't know' without explanation
Doesn't elaborate unless pressed — short sentences, no storytelling
Will sometimes answer with just a few words
Comfortable with silence — doesn't fill space with words

expected_answer_style.q1_business_identity: Morlock Landscape and Design, Naperville, Illinois. Landscaping — maintenance, design-build, hardscaping, outdoor kitchens, fire pits, pavers, the whole thing. Family-owned, 35 years.
expected_answer_style.q2_primary_challenge: Winter kills us. We're booked solid April through October, turning down work. November to March I'm barely covering overhead. We do fall cleanup but that only stretches to early December.
expected_answer_style.q3_ideal_customer: Homeowner, nice property, Naperville or Aurora or Wheaton area. Wants quality, not the cheapest bid. Hires us for maintenance first, then the bigger projects come — the patio, the outdoor kitchen, the fire pit. Those design-build jobs are $15,000-50,000.
expected_answer_style.q4_website_observation: Yeah, I know. It needs work.
expected_answer_style.q5_failed_attempts: Door hangers, once. 2,000 of them. Got maybe 5 calls. That's about it.
expected_answer_style.q6_channel_economics: Repeat customers. Referrals. Some Google. Nextdoor helps — people ask for landscaper recommendations and our name comes up. A couple real estate agents send us stuff.
expected_answer_style.q7_budget_or_retention: I don't know. What do businesses like mine usually spend?
expected_answer_style.q8_execution_capacity: Depends on the season. I don't know, a couple hours a week maybe.
expected_answer_style.q9_success_definition: Fill the winter. Get to $15,000-20,000 a month November through March instead of $5,000-8,000.
